info.title: Predict REST API
info.summary: REST API proxy for Predict.fun prediction marketplace

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Authentication endpoints for obtaining JWT tokens

get.description:
Get the message to sign to generate a JWT token.
Do not hardcode this message as it is dynamic and can be subject to changes.

400.description: Bad request (400) - Invalid input or parameters

403.description: Forbidden (403) - Valid auth but insufficient permissions

410.description: Gone (410) - Resource has been permanently removed

429.description: Too many requests (429) - Rate limit exceeded

post.description: Provide a signature and get back a valid JWT that allows order actions.

post.description:
Remove orders from the orderbook for the authenticated signer (max 100).
This will not cancel the order on-chain.
Requires JWT or project authentication. Project auth requires an
EIP-712 signature in the `x-signature` header.

get.description:
Returns orderbooks for up to 100 markets in a single call.
Markets without an orderbook are omitted from the response.

410.description: Gone (410) - Resource has been permanently removed

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the time range (unix seconds, inclusive)

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the time range (unix seconds, inclusive), defaults to now

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of data points to return (1-1000), defaults to 150

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination from a previous response

get.description:
Fetch all available translations for a category, its markets, and their
outcomes for the requested languages in a single call.

`languages` is a comma-separated list of BCP 47 language codes
(e.g. `zh-CN,ja-JP,ko-KR`). If a language code has no completed
translations it is omitted from the response rather than returning an error.

Get the Merkle proof and claimable amounts for the authenticated user.
Returns `null` data when no unclaimed proof exists.

Requires JWT authentication.

fee.description: The fee for the order, only present for MATCH_SUCCESS events

allOf.description: The fee for the order, only present for MATCH_SUCCESS events

message.description: The message to sign with your wallet

BulletinBoardUpdate.title: BulletinBoardUpdate
BulletinBoardUpdate.description: A single bulletin board update for a market.

CategoryTranslationData.title: CategoryTranslationData
CategoryTranslationData.description: All translated content for one language.

CategoryTranslationFields.title: CategoryTranslationFields
CategoryTranslationFields.description: Translated fields for the category itself.

translations.description: Map from language code (e.g. `"zh-CN"`) to its translations.

CategoryWithStats.title: CategoryWithStats
CategoryWithStats.description: Category data with optional inline statistics.

shortTitle.description: Short, human-readable label, e.g. `"BTC Up or Down 15m"`. `null` when not available.

teams.description: Teams playing in this sports event, when applicable.

isMinAmountOut.description:
Signals that `takerAmount` is deflated and represents a signed minimum,
not the actual fill target. When `true` the backend derives the actual
share quantity from the expected cost:
`amount = makerAmount * 1e18 / priceInCurrency`.

startPrice.description: Starting price; null before market start

endPrice.description: Ending price from Pyth oracle, null if not yet resolved

priceFeedSymbol.description: Price feed symbol (e.g. "BTCUSDT" for Binance, "BTC_USD" for Pyth)

ErrorCode.description: Machine-readable error codes for all API error responses.

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
ErrorResponse.description:
Structured error response body.

Every error path in the API produces this shape.

error.description:
Stable machine-readable error code for client-side matching.

For 400 responses this is the granular bad-request code (e.g.
`create_order_insufficient_collateral_allowance`). For all other
responses it is the HTTP-status-derived code (e.g. `unauthorized`).

allOf.description:
Stable machine-readable error code for client-side matching.

For 400 responses this is the granular bad-request code (e.g.
`create_order_insufficient_collateral_allowance`). For all other
responses it is the HTTP-status-derived code (e.g. `unauthorized`).

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of when the error occurred

trace.description: Trace ID for correlating with server logs

type.description: The fee side, either COLLATERAL (e.g. USDT) or SHARES

allOf.description: The fee side, either COLLATERAL (e.g. USDT) or SHARES

Market.title: Market
Market.description: Full market data as returned by the API

team.description: Set when this entire market is about a single team (SportsMatch team-win markets).

allOf.description: Set when this entire market is about a single team (SportsMatch team-win markets).

current.description: Active period at request time, or `null` if no rewards are accruing.

allOf.description: Active period at request time, or `null` if no rewards are accruing.

schedule.description: Full schedule of past and future reward periods for this market.

MarketTradingStatus.description: Trading status derived from market visibility and deletion state.

MarketTranslationData.title: MarketTranslationData
MarketTranslationData.description: Translated fields for a single market.

MarketVariant.description: Market variant type (DB `category.market_variant_id` values as repr).

MarketWithStats.title: MarketWithStats
MarketWithStats.description: Market data with optional inline statistics.

team.description: Set when this entire market is about a single team (SportsMatch team-win markets).

allOf.description: Set when this entire market is about a single team (SportsMatch team-win markets).

OrderData.title: OrderData
OrderData.description: Order data as returned by the API

OrderFillData.title: OrderFillData
OrderFillData.description: Order fill data (for maker/taker in matches)

OrderStatusFilter.description: Order status filter for queries (repr values match `order_status.id`).

bestBid.description: `None` for resolved outcomes or when the side is empty.

allOf.description: `None` for resolved outcomes or when the side is empty.

bestAsk.description: `None` for resolved outcomes or when the side is empty.

allOf.description: `None` for resolved outcomes or when the side is empty.

team.description: Set when this outcome represents a specific team (SportsTeamMatch).

allOf.description: Set when this outcome represents a specific team (SportsTeamMatch).

OutcomeSide.description: Outcome side (Yes or No). DB stores as bool: true = Yes, false = No.

OutcomeStatus.description: Outcome resolution status. DB stores as bool: true = Won, false = Lost.

OutcomeTranslation.title: OutcomeTranslation
OutcomeTranslation.description: Translated fields for a single outcome.

PendingYieldData.title: PendingYieldData
PendingYieldData.description: Pending yield data including Merkle proof

data.description: Pending yield data, or null when no unclaimed proof exists

allOf.description: Pending yield data, or null when no unclaimed proof exists

QuoteType.description: Quote type (Ask or Bid). DB stores as bool: true = Ask, false = Bid.

noop.description: Orders that were not removed because they were already filled, removed or cancelled

ResolutionProvider.description: Resolution provider for a category (maps to `resolution_provider_type` table).

RewardPeriod.title: RewardPeriod
RewardPeriod.description: A single span during which a market accrues rewards at a fixed hourly rate.

hourlyRate.description: Reward points earned per hour while this period is active.

TimeseriesBatchData.title: TimeseriesBatchData
TimeseriesBatchData.description: `resolution` is shared across all markets in the request.

TimeseriesData.title: TimeseriesData
TimeseriesData.description: Inner data for the timeseries response

resolution.description: The resolution of the returned data points

allOf.description: The resolution of the returned data points

series.description: Data points ordered by time ascending

TimeseriesLatestData.title: TimeseriesLatestData
TimeseriesLatestData.description: Inner data for the latest timeseries response

tweetCount.description: Current count of tweets within the category date range

VariantData.description:
Variant-specific metadata, discriminated by the `type` field.

Known variant types are deserialized into typed structs. Unknown variant
types are handled gracefully by the [`deserialize_variant_data`] helper
on parent structs so that new upstream types don't break deserialization.

VariantData_CryptoUpDownVariantData.description:
Variant-specific metadata, discriminated by the `type` field.

Known variant types are deserialized into typed structs. Unknown variant
types are handled gracefully by the [`deserialize_variant_data`] helper
on parent structs so that new upstream types don't break deserialization.

VariantData_TweetCountVariantData.description:
Variant-specific metadata, discriminated by the `type` field.

Known variant types are deserialized into typed structs. Unknown variant
types are handled gracefully by the [`deserialize_variant_data`] helper
on parent structs so that new upstream types don't break deserialization.

ApiKeyAuth.description: API key authentication via the `x-api-key` header.
ApiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key

BearerAuth.description: Bearer authentication via the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
